mechanism: Electric/compressed-air horn producing sustained low-frequency tone at regular intervals
season: Fog (primarily late spring through early fall)

admiralty_inlet.function: Primary channel for all deep-draft vessel traffic entering/exiting Puget Sound

point_wilson_lighthouse.fog_signal_type: Electric fog horn (automated), triggers on visibility reduction below threshold
point_wilson_lighthouse.significance: Critical navigational turning point: change course from Strait of Juan de Fuca into Admiralty Inlet

fog_horn_technology.mechanism: Steam plant drives air through large horn
Compressed air through slotted reciprocating piston
fog_horn_technology.signature: Two-tone blast with characteristic 'grunt' at end

fog_horn_technology.mechanism: Diaphragm vibrated by compressed air or steam
Electric air horn or electronic oscillator
fog_horn_technology.note: Automated with Point Wilson (1977). Triggers on visibility threshold.

acoustic_properties.frequency_hz: 100-1000 Hz characteristic; moan quality in 200-400 Hz range
acoustic_properties.frequency_rationale: Low frequency propagates farther through fog and turbulent air than high frequency
acoustic_properties.effective_range_miles: 5-10 in dense fog for major lighthouse signals
acoustic_properties.signal_codes: Interval, duration, blasts per sequence identify station. Published in USCG light lists.
acoustic_properties.e3_placement: Present and clearly audible at distance. Periodic, not continuous. Moderate energy.
acoustic_properties.moan_quality: Sustained low note, several seconds, then silence, then again. Associated in literature with isolation, danger, and sublime uncertainty.

cultural_references.ray_bradbury: 'The Fog Horn' (1951): prehistoric sea creature drawn to lighthouse fog horn, mistaking it for call of another of its kind. The definitive literary treatment of fog horn emotional content.
cultural_references.cultural_embedding: Fog horn as ambient sonic identity of maritime port communities. Part of the place-sound of Port Townsend.
literary_associations: Sublime uncertainty of navigating in reduced visibility

port_townsend.function: Customs port of entry (first point of arrival from Pacific)
port_townsend.1880s_expectation: Expected to become dominant PNW city; railroad connection never arrived; Seattle grew instead
port_townsend.preservation: Victorian commercial buildings preserved by economic stagnation
maritime_culture: Port Townsend Wooden Boat Festival (one of largest wooden boat festivals in US)
Ambient fog horn as town sonic identity

fog_patterns.source: Marine layer fog from Pacific, moves through Strait of Juan de Fuca westerly
fog_patterns.visibility_conditions: Dense marine layer: near-zero visibility. Temperature inversions can trap fog layer.

theory_nodes.concept: MARITIME FAMILY ENTERS SPS ENGINE (degree 254). Fog Horn is the first entry in the Maritime family. Previous families: Hydrology (tidal sounds), Meteorology (wind, rain), Biology (species sounds). Maritime family will document human maritime infrastructure: fog horns, ship horns, port sounds.

Diaphone (John Philp Northey, Canada, 1907): the fog horn technology of cultural memory. Two-tone blast with characteristic 'grunt' at end. Produced by compressed air through slotted reciprocating piston. The sound that appears in maritime literature and film.
theory_nodes.description: Diaphone: the definitive fog horn technology.

theory_nodes.concept: Fog horn frequency rationale: 200-400 Hz optimal for fog propagation. Low frequency atmospheric absorption is lower than high frequency. The moan is not aesthetic choice but acoustic physics: low pitch = long range in fog.
theory_nodes.description: Fog horn frequency: acoustic physics of atmospheric propagation.

cross_references.relationship: Both at Admiralty Inlet. The tidal current flows through the inlet (E=2, infrasonic); the fog horn signals across it (E=3, audible). Natural hydraulics and human maritime safety infrastructure at the same geographic point.
253-255 infrastructure arc: water (tidal current) → maritime safety (fog horn) → working forest (log truck). Human and natural infrastructure sequence.

s36_pair.pairing_note: E=8 post-hardcore vs E=3 maritime warning. Both are warning signals: Botch warns that something demanding is coming (adjust your perceptual tools); the fog horn warns ships that something solid is nearby (slow down, navigate carefully). Warning systems at the threshold of ordinary perception. Both demand a response.

milestones: ADMIRALTY INLET documented as primary PNW maritime channel
